endpoints.name: Fetch all builds of an account.
endpoints.description: Fetch all builds of an account. You can limit the number of records and apply filter on status,build date range and sort by users,start date and end date in asc and desc order. You can apply sort on multiple columns.

auth.name: Authorization
auth.description: Basic authentication header of the form `Basic <encoded-value>`, where `<encoded-value>` is the base64-encoded string `username:password`.

queryParams.name: offset
queryParams.description: It defines the number of lists on the basis of limit parameter. e.g offset=10

queryParams.name: limit
queryParams.description: To fetch specified number of records. e.g. limit=10

queryParams.name: status
queryParams.description: To fetch the list of builds with specific statuses. You can pass multiple comma seperated statuses e.g. running,queued,completed,timeout and error.

queryParams.name: fromdate
queryParams.description: To fetch the list of builds that executed from the specified Start Date. The Date format must be YYYY-MM-DD. e.g. "2018-03-15".

queryParams.name: todate
queryParams.description: To fetch the list of builds that executed till the specified End Date. If both fromdate and todate value provided then it works as range filter. The Date format must be YYYY-MM-DD. e.g. "2018-03-15".

queryParams.name: sort
queryParams.description: To sort the list in ascending or descending order using multiple keys. e.g. "asc.user_id,desc.org_id"

queryParams.name: publicurl
queryParams.description: If true, includes the public URL for each build in the response. If false (default), public URLs are omitted. When true, the limit cannot exceed 20.

queryParams.name: username
queryParams.description: To filter builds by username(s). You can pass a single username or multiple comma-separated usernames (e.g. "alice,bob").

For every pre run action you need to upload 2 scripts (Pre run file and Post run file). endpoints.description: This API retrieves scenario-level execution details for a given Job ID. A scenario in HyperExecute represents a logical stage or group of test cases executed within a [**Job**](https://www.lambdatest.com/support/docs/hyperexecute-guided-walkthrough/#jobs-page). Each job consists of multiple tasks, and each task may contain one or more scenarios.

endpoints.name: Retrieve a summary of performance testing results for a specific job.
endpoints.description: This API provides an overview of performance test results for a given job, including job ID, execution time, duration, request count, bandwidth, throughput, response times, ramp time, and user count. It helps assess the system's overall performance and scalability.

auth.name: Authorization
auth.description: Basic authentication header of the form `Basic <encoded-value>`, where `<encoded-value>` is the base64-encoded string `username:password`.

400.description: Bad request error occurred while processing the request.

endpoints.name: Fetch detailed request statistics for a performance test within a specific job.
endpoints.description: This API retrieves detailed performance data for requests made during a test, including throughput, bandwidth, response times, and error counts, helping identify bottlenecks and analyze system performance under load.

auth.name: Authorization
auth.description: Basic authentication header of the form `Basic <encoded-value>`, where `<encoded-value>` is the base64-encoded string `username:password`.

queryParams.name: limit
queryParams.description: Maximum number of records to return. Default value is 100

queryParams.name: offset
queryParams.description: Number of records to skip before starting to return. Default-0, Max - 10,000

queryParams.name: sortBy
queryParams.description: Sort the records by a specific field.

queryParams.name: order
queryParams.description: Sort order for the records.

queryParams.name: timeRange
queryParams.description: Time range for filtering records in the format "startTimestamp,endTimestamp" (e.g., 2023-01-01T00:00:00,2023-02-01T00:00:00).

queryParams.name: timeZone
queryParams.description: Time zone for filtering records. Must be in the format [+/-]HH:mm. Required when timeRange is provided. Example: +05:30
